title: Mr. Phillips, very talkative substitute — Walmart/Costco/Disney lessons
html:
TL;DR

  • Yahir, age 16-17, transcribes lessons from a substitute teacher named Mr. Phillips who "lost out on Walmart, Costco, Disney" — a regret-narrated investing tutorial.
  • Three rules captured: dip your toes in water (don't sell the farm and you still get some of the success), compound your shit (dividend reinvestment, "as in rent to buy more properties"), and use knowledge sometime (the unstated vs. just hoarding it).
  • Earliest-extant transcription of an adult-mentor financial aphorism in Yahir's notes — predates the Cassidy aphorisms by 3 years and the formal value investing thesis-questions (2022 09 28 predict future thru past) by 3.5.
  • Sits 12 days after "those with urges tend to lose" and 13 days before the Forsaken for me mortality note — the Mar 2019 cluster is a tight self-discipline / stakes / scarcity triptych.
  • "Compound your shit" becomes the dividend-reinvestment-as-life-policy trope — recurring in the April 2020 custodial-TDA note and the teen era money instinct.

Notes

The substitute-as-mentor pattern is interesting on its own — Yahir takes notes on a talkative substitute teacher's investing-regret monologue and treats it as canonical. This is the same instinct that produces the Cassidy quotes in 2022 and the mentors-from-afar frame later: the source's credentials are less important than whether the line lands.

The Walmart/Costco/Disney triple is the regret-canon — the three "obvious" American compounders that Phillips watched go to the moon without him. Yahir is hearing this story at exactly the right age for it to imprint. His later chick fil a / whole foods / heb watch-list logic (2025 09 23 how im scraping by) follows the same template — find the thing that's already obvious and don't be too cool to buy it.

"Dip your toes in water, that way you don't sell the farm and get a bit of the success" is a Charlie-Munger-grade single-sentence investing rule. The Phillips paraphrase is doing the same work as the Chamath / Cuban / Frankl one-liners that populate later notes — Yahir's brain runs on aphorism caching.

"Rent to buy more properties" is the real-estate translation of dividend reinvestment, which suggests Phillips was either a part-time landlord or just used the metaphor to bridge the abstract. It's also the seed of the mom house by the river aspiration — property as compounding vehicle, not just shelter.

Privacy note

"Mr. Phillips" is a non-public-figure (a substitute teacher) but he's already in the raw vault and the references are non-defamatory. Stub creation deferred — single-doc mention, queue to Potential Entities.

Open questions

  • Did Yahir act on any of these rules in 2019? The Brokers-and-custodial-TDA chronology starts in April 2020, 13 months later — Phillips may be the proximate cause.
  • Is the "use knowledge sometime" line a Phillips quote or a Yahir self-instruction? The shift to imperative voice suggests the latter.

Connections

  • 2019 03 12 those with urges tend to lose · 2019 03 27 mom said son of gtanos client died within a week of getting — March 2019 sibling notes
  • 2020 04 23 go to diamond — first executed value-investing follow-through
  • james cassidy · viktor frankl · — same aphorism-caching pattern
  • grants pass teen era · value investing · mom house by the river goal · bridge income strategies
